So: invariance != universal impossibility -- and the constraint_expansion INSTANCE rows (CE-H/CE-J) present it as universal impossibility without that narrowing.

overall.bottom_line: P1's constraint_expansion inventory is substantially accurate: every committed-artifact number reproduces exactly, the 259->92 category-error correction is VERIFIED and correctly scoped (necessary-not-sufficient), and verdict semantics are almost entirely correct. The single genuine defect is that the CE-H/CE-J instance rows present the relabeling-invariance result as a GLOBAL provable-impossibility theorem, whereas P1's OWN LIN-05 lineage records that theorem as SUPERSEDED/OVERSTATED by the foundry campaign (invariance != universal impossibility). This is an internal inconsistency between P1's instance layer and lineage layer, partially mitigated by the CE-L note. Secondary: ~7/20 rows are meta/audit/gate/control not novel LA methods (correctly labeled), and CE-E's controls risk cross-scope double-count (disclosed).
